hypothesis: At fixed wall-clock inference latency, MLIP architectures that reallocate compute budget from higher-order equivariant tensor features (increased l_max) to deeper message-passing and larger scalar channel widths will produce more accurate long-timescale thermodynamic observables (e.g., free-energy differences and folding/unfolding rates) in biomolecular MD than architectures that use the same latency to increase equivariant order.
